actions: Powerfully tonifies Primal Qi (Yuan Qi)
Strengthens Spleen and tonifies Stomach
Tonifies Lungs and augments Qi
Generates fluids and stops thirst
Calms the Spirit (Shen) and improves mental function
contraindications: Incompatible with Li Lu. Antagonizes Wu Ling Zhi. Avoid in Excess Heat, Yin deficiency with Heat, or early stages of external pathogens.

actions: Tonifies Qi and raises Yang
Augments the Protective Qi (Wei Qi) and stabilizes the exterior
Promotes urination and reduces edema
Promotes the discharge of pus and generates flesh
contraindications: Avoid in Yin deficiency with Yang rising, early stages of sores, or Excess Heat.

meridians: All 12 Meridians, principally Heart, Lung, Spleen, Stomach

actions: Tonifies Spleen and augments Qi
Moistens Lungs and stops cough
Moderates spasms and alleviates pain
Harmonizes the characteristics of other herbs (Envoy)
contraindications: Incompatible with Gan Sui, Da Ji, Hai Zhao, and Yuan Hua (18 Incompatibilities). Avoid with Dampness or edema.

actions: Tonifies Blood and regulates menstruation
Invigorates Blood and disperses cold
Moistens the Intestines and unblocks the bowels
contraindications: Avoid in cases of diarrhea or abdominal fullness due to Dampness.

actions: Nourishes Blood
Nourishes Kidney Yin
Boosts Jing (Essence) and fills the Marrow
contraindications: Highly cloying. Contraindicated in Qi stagnation, profuse phlegm, or poor digestion.

actions: Nourishes Blood and regulates menses
Calms Liver Yang and alleviates pain
Preserves Yin and adjusts the Ying and Wei levels
contraindications: Incompatible with Li Lu. Use caution with Cold/Deficiency diarrhea.

actions: Invigorates Blood and promotes the movement of Qi
Expels Wind and alleviates pain (especially headaches)
contraindications: Avoid in Yin deficiency with Fire, or excessive menstrual bleeding.

actions: Resolves Lesser Yang (Shao Yang) disorders
Spreads Liver Qi and relieves constraint
Raises Yang Qi in Spleen and Stomach deficiency
contraindications: Avoid in severe Yin deficiency with Liver Yang rising.

actions: Promotes urination and leaches out Dampness
Strengthens the Spleen
Quiets the Heart and calms the Spirit (Shen)
contraindications: Avoid in copious, frequent urination due to Deficiency Cold.

actions: Tonifies Spleen and augments Qi
Dries Dampness and promotes water metabolism
Stabilizes the exterior and stops sweating
Calms the fetus
contraindications: Avoid in Yin deficiency with Heat, or severe fluid depletion.

actions: Regulates Qi and adjusts the Middle
Dries Dampness and transforms Phlegm
Helps prevent stagnation from cloying tonic herbs
contraindications: Use caution with dry cough from Qi or Yin deficiency.

flavor: Toxic (when raw, neutralized by Zhi prep)

actions: Dries Dampness and transforms Phlegm
Descends rebellious Qi and stops vomiting
Dissolves nodules and dissipates clumps
contraindications: Incompatible with Wu Tou. Avoid in dry cough from Yin deficiency or bleeding disorders.

actions: Moistens the Lungs and nourishes Yin
Augments Stomach Yin and generates fluids
Clears Heart Heat and eliminates irritability
contraindications: Avoid in Spleen deficiency with loose stools or Damp-Phlegm.

actions: Nourishes and tonifies Liver and Kidney Yin
Benefits Jing (Essence) and brightens the eyes
Moistens the Lungs
contraindications: Avoid with external pathogenic heat or Spleen dampness with diarrhea.

actions: Releases the Exterior and disperses Cold
Warms the Middle and stops vomiting
Warms the Lungs and stops cough
Reduces the toxicity of other herbs
contraindications: Contraindicated in spontaneous sweating from Qi deficiency and Heat signs.

actions: Tonifies Spleen and Stomach Qi
Nourishes the Blood and calms the Spirit
Moderates and harmonizes the harsh properties of other herbs
contraindications: Avoid in cases of Damp-Phlegm, food stagnation, or excessive Heat.

actions: Tonifies Liver and Kidney, nourishes Blood, and boosts Jing
Secures Essence and stops leakage
Moistens Intestines and unblocks bowels
contraindications: Monitor liver enzymes in susceptible individuals. Avoid in Spleen deficiency with dampness.

actions: Clears Heat and resolves toxicity
Vents and disperses externally contracted Wind-Heat
Clears Damp-Heat from the lower burner
contraindications: Avoid in cases of Deficiency Cold of the Spleen and Stomach.

actions: Invigorates Blood and dispels stasis
Clears Heat and soothes irritability
Nourishes the Blood and calms the Spirit
contraindications: Incompatible with Li Lu. Exercise caution for patients on blood-thinning medications.

actions: Tonifies Qi and Yin of the Spleen and Stomach
Tonifies Lung Qi and augments Lung Yin
Tonifies the Kidneys and stabilizes Essence (Jing)
contraindications: Use caution in cases of Excess Heat or accumulation of Dampness.

actions: Opens up and disseminates Lung Qi, dispels Phlegm
Benefits the throat
Directs the effects of other herbs to the upper body (Envoy)
contraindications: Avoid in chronic cough from Yin deficiency or in cases of hemoptysis.

actions: Fortifies Kidney Yang and warms the Ming Men Fire
Disperses deep Cold and warms the channels
Leads the Fire back to its source
contraindications: Incompatible with Chi Shi Zhi. Avoid during pregnancy, or in Yin deficiency with Fire.

actions: Disperses Wind-Heat, clears and benefits the head and eyes
Vents rashes
Allows constrained Liver Qi to flow freely
contraindications: Avoid in cases of Yin deficiency with Yang rising, or nursing mothers (can decrease lactation).

actions: Clears Heat and drains Fire (especially in the Qi level)
Clears Heat from the Lungs and Stomach
Quenches thirst and relieves irritability
contraindications: Avoid in cases of Cold from deficiency of Spleen/Stomach, or Yang deficiency.

actions: Contains the leakage of Lung Qi and stops cough
Tonifies the Kidneys and binds Essence (Jing)
Generates fluids and quiets the Spirit
contraindications: Avoid in cases of exterior conditions not fully cleared, or early stages of cough/rash.
